briefdescription.para: Maximum message size.
The size of the log buffer.
1sec encryption status check interval
2sec RepeaterAuth Ack interval
HDCP22 over HDMI.
HDCP22 over DP, Not yet supported.
These constants are used to define the protocol.
HDCP22 receiver.
HDCP22 repeater upstream interface.
HDCP22 converter upstream interface.
These constants are used to define the mode.
Undefined.
Set the DDC register address.
Set the DDC register data.
Get the DDC register data.
Get the DDC the write buffer size.
Get the DDC the read buffer size.
Check if the DDC write buffer is empty.
Check if the DDC read buffer is empty.
Clear the DDC read buffer.
Clear the DDC write buffer.
Executed when state machine transitions to authenticated.
Executed when state machine transitions to unauthenticated.
Executed when authentication request is received.
Executed when state machine is ready for the topology update.
Executed when stream management request is received.
Executed when encryption status update.
Invalid.
These constants are used to identify callback functions.
Undefined.
Unauthenticated.
Compute Km: Send AKE_Send_Cert.
Compute Km: Wait for AKE_No_Stored_km or AKE_Stored_km.
Compute Km: Send AKE_Send_H_prime.
Compute Km: Send AKE_Send_Pairing_Info.
Compute Km: Wait for LCInit.
Compute L': Send LC_Send_L_prime.
Compute L': Wait for SKE_Send_Eks.
Compute Ks.
Receiver Authenticated.
Wait for Downstream.
Assemble Receiver ID List: Send RepeaterAuth_Send_ReceiverID_List.
Assemble Receiver ID List: Send RepeaterAuth_Send_ReceiverID_List Done.
Verify Receiver ID List Acknowledgement.
Content Stream Management: Wait for RepeaterAuth_Stream_Manage.
Content Stream Management: Send RepeaterAuth_Stream_Ready.
Content Stream Management: Send RepeaterAuth_Stream_Ready Done.
Repeater Authenticated.
Invalid.
These constants are the authentication and key exchange states.
Unauthenticated.
Authentication Busy.
Authenticated.
Reauthentication requested.
These constants define the authentication status.
Log Event None.
Log General Info Event.
Log State Info Event.
Log Message Info Event.
Log Debug Event.
Log Error Event.
User logging.

detaileddescription.para: Last value the list, only used for checking

briefdescription.para: These constants are the general logging events.
These constants are used to identify fields inside the topology structure.
Type for pointer to state function.
Type for pointer to single input function.
Type for pointer to two input function.
Type for pointer to single input function with a return value.
This function returns a reference to an XHdcp22_Rx_Config structure based on the core id, DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xhdcp22_rx_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique core ID of the HDCP RX core for the lookup operation.

simplesect.para: XHdcp22Rx_LookupConfig returns a reference to a config record in the configuration table (in xhdmi_tx_g.c) corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the instance provided by the caller based on the given configuration data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to an XHdcp22_Rx instance. The memory the pointer references must be pre-allocated by the caller. Further calls to manipulate the driver through the HDCP22-RX API must be made with this pointer.

is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific HDCP22-RX device. This function initializes an InstancePtr object for a specific device specified by the contents of Config. This function can initialize multiple instance objects with the use of multiple calls giving different Config information on each call.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the HDCP22-RX state machine.

detaileddescription.para: The HDCP2Version register is set to active.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: Before enabling the state machine ensure that the instance has been initialized, DDC message handles have been assigned, and keys have been loaded.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the HDCP22-RX state machine.

detaileddescription.para: The HDCP2Version register is cleared, and the ReauthReq bit is set in the RxStatus register to allow the transmitter to recover when it has already authenticated. Without setting the ReauthReq bit the transmitter will not know that HDCP 2.2 protocol has been disabled by the Receiver after it has already authenticated.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the HDCP22-RX system to the default state.

detaileddescription.para: The HDCP22-RX DDC registers are set to their default value and the message buffer is reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: The DDC message handles must be assigned by the XHdcp22Rx_SetDdcHandles function prior to calling this reset function.

briefdescription.para: This function executes the HDCP22-RX state machine.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function installs callback functions for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ------------------------- --------------------------- (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_DDC_SETREGADDR) DdcSetAddressCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_DDC_SETREGDATA) DdcSetDataCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_DDC_GETREGDATA) DdcGetDataCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_DDC_GETWBUFSIZE) DdcGetWriteBufferSizeCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_DDC_GETRBUFSIZE) DdcGetReadBufferSizeCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_DDC_ISWBUFEMPTY) DdcIsWriteBufferEmptyCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_DDC_ISRBUFEMPTY) DdcIsReadBufferEmptyCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_DDC_CLEARRBUF) DdcClearReadBufferCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_DDC_CLEARWBUF) DdcClearWriteBufferCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_AUTHENTICATED) AuthenticatedCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_UNAUTHENTICATED) UnauthenticatedCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_AUTHENTICATION_REQUEST) AuthenticationRequestCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_STREAM_MANAGE_REQUEST) StreamManageRequestCallback (XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_TOPOLOGY_UPDATE) TopologyUpdateCallback

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX core instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the version.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: Returns the version register of the cipher.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to the internal timer control instance needed for connecting the timer interrupt to an interrupt controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the internal timer control instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the HDCP22-RX state machine is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the HDCP22-RX cipher encryption is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the HDCP22-RX state machine is enabled but not yet in the Authenticated state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the HDCP22-RX state machine is in the Authenticated state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the HDCP22-RX state machine has detected an error condition.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the state machine information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when 50 consecutive data island ECC errors are detected indicating a link integrity problem.

detaileddescription.para: The error flag is set indicating to the state machine that REAUTH_REQ bit in the RXSTATUS register be set. Setting this flag only takes effect when the authentication state machine is in the Authenticated state B4.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: This function can be registered as an asynchronous callback.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a DDC read/write burst stops prior to completing the expected message size.

detaileddescription.para: This will cause the message buffers to be flushed and state machine reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: This function can be registered as an asynchronous callback.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a complete message is available in the write message buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: This function can be registered as an asynchronous callback.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a message has been read out of the read message buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: This function can be registered as an asynchronous callback.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to load the Lc128 value by copying the contents of the array referenced by Lc128Ptr into the cipher.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.
is a pointer to an array.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to load the public certificate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.
is a pointer to an array.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to load the private key.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.
is a pointer to an array.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if MMULT keys are calculated correctly.
XST_FAILURE if MMULT keys are not calculated correctly.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the log pointers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.
allows to add debug logging.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the time expired since a log reset was called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: The expired logging time in useconds.

briefdescription.para: This function writes HDCP22-RX log event into buffer.

detaileddescription.para: If the log event is of type error, the sticky error flag is set. The sticky error flag is used to keep history of error conditions.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.
specifies an action to be carried out.
specifies the information that gets written into log buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function provides the log information from the log buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

listitem.para: Content of log buffer if log pointers are not equal.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the contents of the log buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function copies a complete repeater topology table into the instance repeater topology table.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.
is a pointer to the repeater topology table.

briefdescription.para: This function copies the RECEIVER_ID_LIST into the repeater topology table.

detaileddescription.para: Receiver ID list is constructed by appending Receiver IDs in big-endian order.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.
is a pointer to the Receiver ID list. The list consists of a contiguous set of bytes stored in big-endian order, with each Receiver ID occuping five bytes with a maximum of 31 Receiver IDs.
is the number of Receiver IDs in the list. The list size cannot exceed 31 devices.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set various fields inside the topology structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.
indicates what field of the topology structure to update.
is the value assigned to the field of the topology structure.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to indicate that the topology table has been updated and is ready for upstream propagation.

detaileddescription.para: Before calling this function ensure that all the fields in the repeater topology table have been updated.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the repeater mode status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.
is TRUE to enable repeater mode and FALSE to disable.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current repeater mode status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the HDCP 2.2 instance is the upstream port of a repeater
FALSE if the HDCP 2.2 instance is a receiver

briefdescription.para: This function is gets the type information received from the RepeaterAuth_Stream_Manage message for downstream propagation of management information.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called only after the RepeaterAuth_Stream_Manage message has been received, and can be called inside the user callback function type XHDCP22_RX_HANDLER_STREAM_MANAGE_REQUEST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para:
- 0x00: Type 0 Content Stream. May be transmitted by the HDCP Repeater to all HDCP Devices.
0x01: Type 1 Content Stream. Must not be transmitted by the HDCP Repeater to HDCP 1.x-compliant Devices and HDCP 2.1-compliant Devices.
0x02-0xFF: Reserved for future use only. Content Streams with reserved Type values must be treated similar to Type 1 Content Streams.
